description_long: This object is a classic triplane airplane with three stacked wings, painted in a deep red color. The fuselage is slender and tapers towards the tail, which features a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, and appears to be open, typical of early 20th-century fighter aircraft. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, arranged in a taildragger configuration. The engine is mounted at the nose, with a visible propeller, indicating a single piston engine setup. The overall design is reminiscent of historic biplanes and triplanes used in early aviation.
description: A deep red triplane with three stacked wings, open cockpit, and a nose-mounted propeller, evoking early 20th-century fighter aircraft.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stacked wings, central propeller, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the stacked wing arrangement, and the taildragger landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly with horizontal stabilizer and single vertical fin, as well as the triplane wing structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the propeller, open cockpit, and the distinctive triple wing configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing; appears to be single-seat with no visible canopy.
